I am a retired business director, married grandfather, and Bodle Street
Green resident. I am standing for the Reform Party because our
county deserves honest leadership and practical solutions that put
people before politics.
We must restore trust, tackle rising costs, and protect the freedoms
and opportunities of our community. I believe in accountability,
common sense, and real change for hardworking citizens. Together,
we can challenge the status quo and create a future that is fairer,
stronger, and more prosperous for everyone.

I’ve been working in East Sussex, from Hastings to Eastbourne, for the last 7 and a half years, as a veterinary surgeon. I’ve also been living in Hailsham/Hellingly for the past 5 years. What I love most about our county are its people and the great natural beauty that the countryside and coastal areas provide us.

Being originally from Portugal, I am happy to call this country my home now. I’ve experienced first hand the hospitality and humanity of the British people and have nothing but good things to say about it. This comes into stark contrast with what we hear in the media. A loud minority would like us to think that the country is divided and are trying to turn neighbour against neighbour. But our day-to-day experiences show us the opposite: a country united by our shared values of peace, freedom, democracy, mutual understanding and the pursuit of happiness; not only for the few, but for all of us!

For too many years, politicians have been disconnected from these shared values. Understandably, people have become disillusioned with politics and feeling that nothing can be changed. Nonetheless, this doesn’t mean that democracy doesn’t work. It simply means that we need better politicians, who closely align with people’s shared values. I believe that the Green Party is bringing back humanity into politics, mirroring British common values.

I’m proud to be the Green Party’s County Councillor candidate for Arlington, East Hoathly & Hellingly. If elected, I will focus my energy and efforts in the following areas:

● Road maintenance: make sure that current maintenance contracts are fit for purpose, delivering good value for money and delivering good performance. I will focus on the motto that it’s better to fix something properly the first time, than multiple times in a lesser manner, to deliver long-term sustainability and reliability for our road infrastructure.

● Community and local businesses: create an online directory of community groups, volunteering opport

With experience in the financial industry and service as an Officer in the Territorial Army, Aidan understands the importance of discipline, accountability, and sensible spending
.
Like many residents, Aidan is deeply concerned about the state of our council’s finances and local services. Reform UK is the only party committed to:

✅ Cost and Efficiency – Focusing on vital services and cutting waste.
✅ Roads and Infrastructure – Calling for proper inspections and high-quality repairs to roads and potholes.
✅ Housing and Planning – Protecting our green spaces and prioritising brownfield development over mass overdevelopment.

Our roads need urgent attention, our council tax is being wasted, and our communities deserve better. The Lib Dems, Tories, and Labour have had their chance — now it’s time for Reform UK to stand up for local people.

🗳️ Vote Aidan Fisher – Reform UK for Ashdown & Conquest
👉 Only Reform UK will stand up for your priorities.

I am standing for Ashdown and Conquest as a local person myself . As councillor for Gensing Ward for Hastings Borough Council already I am going to ensure I’m focused on making sure Hastings gets the attention and investment it deserves. I’m working to hold East Sussex County Council to account — pushing for better-maintained roads, improved public transport, and stronger support for both adult and children’s services. I’ll keep standing up for local residents to make sure Hastings’ needs are properly heard and acted on. #greensgetitdone

I am a proud Hastings resident and IT support professional for a Managed Service Provider right here in Hastings, bringing practical problem-solving and real-world experience to local decision-making.

My focuses are, but not limited to, fixing potholes and improving roads across Hastings and East Sussex, listening and responding to the needs of the community of which I hope to serve, protecting valued green spaces, vehemently opposing development on designated green belt land, and ensuring efficient, accountable council services that deliver real value for local people.

I have worked with the Make a Wish foundation and ran a 10k last year which, collectively, raised almost £10,000 for Children with life threatening conditions, ensuring every child has the opportunity to shine.

Vote for real change from a real person, vote to fix East Sussex. Vote Reform ➡️

I’m Austin Henderson, and I’m standing to represent our community in the ancient town of Battle and surrounding villages because I believe local people deserve a stronger voice, practical leadership, and real accountability from those in office.

I live locally with my wife, young son and beloved dog. Like many families here, I care deeply about the future of our area, from the quality of housing and infrastructure, to the safety, prosperity, and opportunities available to the next generation.

Before entering construction, I served in the British Army with the Royal Engineers. That experience instilled in me discipline, resilience, and a strong sense of duty. These are values I have carried through into my civilian career. I now work as a Project Manager delivering large-scale residential developments, managing teams, budgets, and complex programmes. 
My job is about solving problems, making decisions under pressure, and delivering results. That’s exactly the approach I would bring to public office.

I am standing because I want to see a return to common-sense decision making. That means supporting responsible development that delivers high-quality homes without overburdening local infrastructure, road safety and improved maintenance contracts, backing local businesses and workers, and ensuring that public services are focused on the needs of the people who rely on them.

Like many people, my political views have evolved over time as I’ve gained real-world experience in work, family life, and my community. I believe in being honest about that. What matters to me now is standing up for the interests of local residents, listening properly, and acting with integrity.

This election is an opportunity to bring practical experience and a fresh perspective into local decision-making. I would be honoured to have your support.

I was first elected to Wealden District Council in May 1999. I represented Ninfield and Hooe and the ward has since been expanded and is now served by two councillors. I have acquired a wealth of experience over the years and am as enthusiastic and energetic as ever.  I have always been passionate about supporting and working for the community, giving of my time cheerfully and willingly.  By meeting with and listening to residents I am delighted that I have been able to make a difference.

For the last year I have been the lead member at Wealden for delivering the Council’s Climate Change Action plan, Community and Public Health and Community Leadership and Communications. Subjects important to us all.

With your support I will continue to represent you to the very best of my ability.

Independent Candidate for Bexhill East

A Practical Plan for Our Community

I am standing as an Independent candidate for Bexhill East on East Sussex County Council to put local people first—without party politics, and with a clear focus on delivering real results.

This is a practical plan focused on the issues that matter most in Bexhill: adult social care, housing, education, transport, and our roads.

⸻

Adult Social Care – Urgent Action Needed

Adult social care is one of the biggest challenges facing our area. Bexhill has a growing older population, and many families are already feeling the strain from delays, lack of support, and overstretched services.

Too often, people are waiting too long for assessments, struggling to arrange care, or stepping in themselves when services fall short. This also puts pressure on local NHS services, with people stuck in hospital because care isn’t in place.

My priorities:
	•	Reduce waiting times for care assessments and support packages
	•	Improve access to home care so people can stay independent for longer
	•	Support unpaid carers with better respite services and advice
	•	Push for better coordination between care services and the NHS
	•	Stand up for care workers and highlight staffing shortages
	•	Ensure local concerns are heard and acted on quickly

In Bexhill East, this matters because:
	•	Demand for elderly care is rising
	•	Families are increasingly acting as carers themselves
	•	Local health and care services are under pressure
	•	Delays and gaps in support are affecting real people every day

This is not a distant issue—it is affecting families across our community now, and it needs urgent, practical attention.

⸻

Housing & Social Housing – Homes for Local People

Too many people in Bexhill East are being priced out of the area, while housing developments don’t always reflect local needs.

My priorities:
	•	Increase genuinely affordable housing based on local incomes
	•	Prioritise local residents and key workers

I have lived in Bexhill for the past 15 years and have seen firsthand the devastating impact of cuts to services in the community. Whether it be the lack of decent transport links and school places, the rise in homelessness coupled with cuts to homelessness support or the countless potholes in the road, it is clear our council are unable to provide even the most basic services we need.

This isn't because our council are cruel or incompetent, it is because governments of different parties have spent decades making drastic cuts to local government and attacking public services.

TUSC stands for organising communities and local councils to fight these cuts and propose needs budgets that are drafted based on what communities like Bexhill need rather than passing on cuts from central government.

We are the only party prepared to say we won't pass cuts under any circumstances and we believe in building a party of the working class that will fight for us rather than the super rich!

Donald Walmsley has lived in Bexhill North for twelve years. Before teaching, he spent years in logistics management - running a multi-million pound budget and delivering £8 million in savings over five years.
After 30 years in secondary schools, Donald watched council tax rise and services decline.
In 2024 he co-founded the Bexhill and Battle branch of Reform UK because Bexhill deserves better.
His pledge is personal: to give Bexhill North a real voice, and make transparent, common- sense decisions on behalf of residents.

On Election, Donald is ready to use his wealth of experience to bring the financial discipline and local accountability that Bexhill North deserves.

Our plan to fix East Sussex:

Fix council finances
East Sussex County Council plans to borrow £70 million just to cover day-to-day costs - not to improve services - adding around £3 million a year in interest, with total debt at £211 million despite maximum council tax rises.
Residents who budget and live within their means deserve a council that does the same - properly managed finances and balancing the books.

Cut waste - Spend what remains wisely:
Millions are spent on consultants, administration and management layers that do not deliver front-line services; Reform will review all discretionary spending within the first 90 days and redirect funds to roads, care and useable services.

Fix our roads for good:
East Sussex pays out £ hundreds of thousands in pothole compensation each year because temporary fixes and done instead of proper repairs - costing more in the long term and leaving Bexhill North's roads in ever worsening condition.
We will review the highways contract and its repair specifications, push for more durable, county-wide repairs, and consider all contract options to deliver better roads and better value.

Prioritise what Bexhill North actually needs:
New housing must include the roads, drainage and infrastructure to support it - without it, developers profit while residents face

I am an East Sussex County Councillor and have been a local businessman for three decades. I advocate for local charity fundraising through my business, my tenure as Mayor of Bexhill, and my role as a trustee and member of many charity organizations.

I am currently an East Sussex County Councillor and a former District Councillor.and I have had the privilege of calling Bexhill home for the past 33 years. Since moving to Bexhill with my family, I have been deeply committed to our communitys and its well-being. This passion for local involvement led me to enter politics, proudly representing the Conservative Party, and subsequently serving as Mayor of Bexhill. These roles allowed me to participate in numerous fundraising events, a commitment I continue to uphold. It has been an honor to help raise substantial funds for various causes and support those in need within all our communitys.

My dedication to public service extended to my election to East Sussex County Council, where I have served diligently for several years. However, after careful consideration, I have decided to resign from the Conservative Party. This decision stems from my desire to pursue a more independent political path, one that allows me greater freedom to address the needs and concerns of our residents.  A New Path Forward:  Independent  parliamentery Candidate 

With the recent resignation of our MP, I see this as an a perfect opportunity to stand as an Independent candidate for Bexhill and Battle. I believe that an Independent MP can engage more closely with residents without National Party politics interfering. Fostering genuine conversations about local issues through regular surgeries and face-to-face interactions. Electing an Independent MP would mark a significant turning point, offering a fresh perspective and a renewed focus on the needs of our community. As someone who knows this area intimately and has dedicated over a decade to serving as a Councillor, I am ready to represent you in

Dear Resident
Service has been a constant in my life — first serving
my country in the Army, and now serving my community
in Bexhill.
I currently serve as a Town councillor, working with
residents to tackle local issues and campaigning for
improvements across our town.
Before entering local government, I served for six years
in the Army as a Royal Engineer, including three years
as a Bomb Disposal Engineer in an active UK Search
Team. I am also a Gulf War veteran, and like many
veterans, I live with the long-term physical and mental
effects of that service. My time in the Armed Forces
taught me discipline, responsibility, and the importance
of standing up for others — values that guide my work
today.
My own life experience has also shaped who I am. For
fourteen years of my childhood, through no fault of my
own, I lived in children’s homes across the south of
England. Those years taught me how important stability,
opportunity, and support really are.
That is why I care deeply about communities like Sidley,
one of the most deprived areas in the country. Our
community deserves real support, real investment, and
a strong voice fighting for it at County Hall.

As a candidate for the Labour Party in the forthcoming election, I want to share with you my vision for the future of our community. I believe that we have the opportunity to create a more prosperous, equitable, and sustainable Rother, but to do so, we need bold leadership and a commitment to progress. If elected, I plan to focus on several key issues that are critical to our district's success. These include: * Affordable Housing: I believe that everyone in Rother should have access to safe, affordable housing. We need to work together to address the housing crisis in our district and ensure that our residents have a place to call home. * Economic Development: We need to attract new businesses to Rother and support our existing businesses to create more jobs and opportunities for our residents. I will work tirelessly to promote economic development throughout our district. * Climate Action: Climate change is one of the greatest challenges facing our community and our planet. We need to take bold action to reduce our carbon footprint, protect our natural resources, and build a more sustainable future for our children. But most importantly, I believe in the power of community. As your representative on the Rother District Council, I will work tirelessly to listen to your concerns, advocate for your needs, and build a stronger, more resilient Rother. I ask for your support and your vote in the upcoming election. Together, we can create a better future for all of us.

I was born and raised in Bexhill-on-Sea and have lived here for most of my life. I feel fortunate to know both the town and the surrounding area so well, and I have a strong understanding of the issues that affect our community.I am deeply community-oriented and have been actively involved in bringing people together through events. As an organiser, I successfully delivered large-scale seafront shows, including The Great Gatsby event for two consecutive years, which became a highlight for many residents and visitors alike.My strong sense of fairness and justice has driven me to stand up for local people. I have led three successful petitions on important issues: challenging the excessive £30 parking charge at Camber, protecting public conveniences in Rother, and opposing the removal of flower beds from our beautiful cemetery.In 2021, I was elected to Bexhill Town Council, where I currently serve as Deputy for Finance and Audit. I take pride in representing residents and ensuring their voices are heard. In 2025, I founded The Bexhill Forum, a series of open public meetings that give residents the opportunity to speak openly about serious local issues.It has been a privilege to play a part in shaping the future of our town. While I remain committed to serving Bexhill in the years ahead, I now seek to represent our community at East Sussex County Council. I would be honoured to have your support in the election on May 7th.

**Victoria Carson** lives in Bexhill South and is not a career councillor. She spent three decades building a career in film and television production finance - ending up as a financial controller responsible for supervising production accountants through government audits on multi-million pound projects. She understands exactly how public money should be managed. And she can see that East Sussex County Council has not been managing it well.

As a member of the Rother Forum - which brings residents together directly with the police, local GPs, and charities - Victoria knows what Bexhill residents are dealing with: social care waiting lists that are too long, a seafront and town centre that deserve better investment, parking that is damaging local trade, and a county council that has borrowed £211 million and still cannot fix the roads.

Victoria is standing because the De La Warr Pavilion, the seafront, and Bexhill’s identity as a destination worth visiting deserve a county councillor who will fight for them - and because residents who need care deserve a council that has the financial discipline to fund it properly. A member of the Production Guild of Great Britain, Victoria is a quilt-maker, enthusiastic cook of world cuisine, and a reluctant golfer.

On 7th May, Victoria is ready to put three decades of financial expertise to work for Bexhill South.

I have worked within private, public and academic sectors. As a postman, administrator, commercial-archaeologist, project manager, and now in IT. I've worked alongside charities and communities. I've seen the struggles people face. As a family person with children, we share concerns about worsening conditions. And we too feel the inequality led cost-of-living crisis.

As one of three Rother District Green Party councillors, we know how local government does its best in hard-times. Where we've helped balanced budgets whilst safeguarding people and nature. But only by having more Greens to replace the Conservative led County Council, can we offer real hope and make real change.

Let's have a Bexhill and East Sussex for the people and push back against austerity. Let's improve our future with practical, honest ideas just like we did post-war. Let's have similar renewal now.

NEW EAST SUSSEX UNITARY COUNCIL FORMATION

A new unitary that works for Bexhill and East Sussex's communities is needed. A unitary based on community, wellbeing, arts, culture and nature that represents the best of Bexhill, and the county we live in - East Sussex. A unitary that is not created with austerity in mind but aims to grow and cater for residents needs and aspirations.

EAST SUSSEX COUNTY COUNCIL

Hope for our young & working-age adults.

Skilled jobs, education, activities & engagement from clubs to new businesses. And protecting nature & wildlife- creating a safer Bexhill & Sussex for their future.

Review contracts for the benefit of people not profits.

Whether care, schools, NHS, highways and public transport - those in need should have help. Prioritise worse roads. Look at in-house solutions as contracts end.

Hold an emergency summit to address Tory-led austerity.

Stand against the economic policies of major parties that led to local government decline. Show there is another way. End inequality and the cost-of-living crisis!

Highway Safety, Greening & layout

I am a local business owner, a former Sussex Police Officer and hard-working Rother District Councillor. I know this constituency well. From working in my parent’s shop in Sidley High Street as a kid, to running a B&B on Bexhill seafront with my late husband, Tony. This is my home and I am ready for the chance to show folks in Westminster what being from Bexhill and Battle is all about.

After 14 years of Conservative failures in government, many voters are considering voting Labour, some for the first time in a long time. 

Under Sir Keir Starmer’s leadership, the changed Labour Party is ready to govern and improve things for ordinary people. I am really proud to be standing for Labour at this election with a realistic and achieveable set of priorities that will make a difference to our communities across the Bexhill and Battle constituency. 

Labour can win here, all I need is your support. I promise I will not let you down.

My three priorities for Bexhill and Battle:

Better public services – from public transport and the NHS, to school and nursery provision. I will continue my proven track record of protecting, supporting and improving local public services.

Cleaner, cheaper energy – Renewable, community-led energy generation to bring down prices. I will advocate for our rural, coastal constituency to benefit from Labour’s new GB Energy company.

More jobs for local people – Quality, well-paid jobs to improve the local economy. I will encourage new industry to bring in new jobs, particularly for young people.

VOTE LABOUR Vote for Christine Bayliss on Thursday 4th July

Pete Morley is not a career politician, he has spent years
building and running businesses, managing budgets, employing
local people, and giving back by raising thousands for St. Michael’s
Hospice and the UK Sepsis Trust, as well as volunteering with local
junior football.
A member of the Bexhill Chamber of Commerce, Pete has been
part of the community for decades and understands the challenges
local residents and businesses face.
With the council £211 million in debt and services declining,
Pete is standing to bring financial discipline and accountability. As
a family man with four children, he cares deeply about the future of
Bexhill and wants to see a better future for Bexhill West.
On 7th May, Pete is ready to put his experience to work and
deliver the change residents deserve.
Democracy Restored Democracy Restored
Pete

YOUR LOCAL CANDIDATE FOR YOUR LOCAL CANDIDATE FOR
BEXHILL WEST
Last year, your Conservative and Independent county councillors
voted to cancel the election, denying residents a voice. Reform UK
challenged this in the High Court and restored your vote.
The same councillors, responsible for the council’s financial
crisis, tried to avoid accountability; on 7th May, residents can finally
deliver their verdict.

A Strong, Independent Voice for Bexhill West

Bexhill West deserves a councillor who is truly local, genuinely committed, and prepared to stand up for residents — not someone passing through or following a party line.

I’m Connor Winter, born and raised in Little Common, with deep family roots here going back generations. This isn’t just somewhere I represent — it’s my home. I live here, I work here, and I understand the issues our community faces because I see them every day.

For the past five years, I’ve delivered for residents as an Independent councillor — taking on difficult cases, challenging decisions, and getting real results. From standing up for carers and vulnerable residents, to protecting local services and backing our seafront businesses, my focus has always been the same: putting Bexhill West first.

This election is about who will genuinely represent you at County Hall. East Sussex County Council controls the services that matter most — our roads, schools, and adult social care. Yet too often, decisions are made without proper local understanding, accountability, or even basic communication with residents.

I will bring something different — strong, independent representation that answers to you, not a party machine. I will fight for properly maintained roads, safer streets, fairer social care, and real investment in our community. I will demand better standards, greater transparency, and value for your money.

Others may offer slogans or appear when it suits them. I offer a proven record, year-round visibility, and a clear commitment to this community — because I’m part of it.

On May 7th, choose local. Choose independent. Choose a councillor who will stand up for Bexhill West every single day. Vote Connor Winter.

Becca Horn is a smart and passionate local campaigner and councillor, already working hard on the issues that matter in Hastings & Rye. As a founder member of Hastings & St Leonards Clean Water Action, Becca is known for speaking out to hold Southern Water to account and end sewage pollution. Becca is also involved with Hastings Housing Alliance, working to find community-led solutions to our housing crisis. Hastings is Becca's home town where she works as a community yoga teacher and performer. Becca is dedicated to making it cleaner, greener and safer for future generations to enjoy. Becca says, "My top priorities are: affordable quality homes for all; safe and pleasant town centres connected by better public transport, walking and cycling routes; improving opportunities for young people and cleaning up our rivers and seas by pushing for public water ownership." Becca will be a powerful local voice in Parliament, standing to protect our precious natural environment, preserve our vibrant cultural heritage and speak up for a fairer society for every citizen of Hastings & Rye.

Although born in London, I have lived in Hastings for thirty years. The town has many deep rooted, complex social issues, but I love living here. It has such a vibrant artistic community. The music scene is amazing and of course, it has a unique history.

I am in my third year as a Conservative Councillor for Hastings. It has been an honour to represent Conquest Ward. In that time I have met many diverse people at both ward surgeries and more recently, virtually.

I have a passion for the environment. I would like to see more planning priority put on using the many existing brown field sites in Hastings, rather than the relentless development of our precious green spaces

Hello residents of Westfield, Three Oaks, Broad Oak, Guestling, Sedlescombe, Fairlight, Kent Street, Udimore snd Cock Marling,

I’m Dan Lach and I’d like to introduce myself as your local Reform UK candidate for Brede Valley and Marsham and on behalf of Reform UK I want to personally thank you in advance for your support in the upcoming elections on the 7th of May. 
As a locally based chartered engineer I want to make it my business to get our roads fixed, our local businesses far better supported and a tighter control over council costs and rural housing development.
Visit fixeastsussex.uk to find out more about our plans and policies, including how Reform UK mounted a challenge against Bexhill Town Council which resulted in saving Bexhill residents from an unnecessary 120% rise in their town council tax in 2026.

I am delighted to be seeking re election although in a very difficult time in our history. As a local councillor for both Rother and East Sussex and working with parish councils and community groups I must say I have been truly amazed but not surprised at how well our villagers have helped each other. Well done everyone!  I have grown up in one of our villages in THIS division, went to school here, worked here and I now live with my wife and three children in Westfield; our eldest child is attending the same primary I attended back in the 1970s! All of the villages that make up Brede Valley and Marsham have similar common issues but vary widely in their character.  Throughout my life as a councillor- over 20 years- I have a proven record for dealing with resident’s issues quickly, honestly and efficiently. Even when it isn’t strictly a council matter I will do my best to point you in the right direction. I’ve helped on a variety of projects in our villages; from large grant applications to lowering speed limits and speaking up for residents at all important planning meetings.  I will always put residents first and party politics second. I simply like to get things done for residents working closely with community groups and our parish councils! I hope you will support me on 6th May. Thank you .

Crowborough is unrecognisable from when I moved here 23 years ago.
I’m Catherine, and I joined Reform because I’m dismayed by the damage
caused by successive governments, both nationally and locally. Their lack of
care has impacted daily life and the places we call home.
Overdevelopment of our green spaces without proper scrutiny harms our
wildlife and environment. Rising anti-social behaviour and a limited police
presence weakens our sense of community. Our High Street struggles with
constant turnover, driven by high business rates and a lack of clear direction.
The suspension of birthing services at Crowborough Hospital has also
caused real concern for local families.
East Sussex must stand proud. I will serve Crowborough North and Jarvis
Brook with the residents, listening and working together to restore common
sense, community spirit, and above all accountability.

Jeannette moved to Crowborough from Tunbridge Wells fifteen years ago and has worked to improve the lives of local people ever since. She is working closely with Nus Ghani and Crowborough Shield to fight the Home Office’s plans at Crowborough Army Camp and is out stewarding the marches most Sundays. Jeannette is also working with Nus Ghani to push for the reopening of the Birthing Unit at Crowborough Hospital and for the MIU to be sustainably staffed.

Jeannette has worked with local people and the CPRE to fight planning applications on green field sites, particularly focusing on the damage to fragile habitats. She also worked with local people to try to turn the old lavatory block in the car park at Jarvis Brook into a Community Hub and is very pleased that Southern Rail has now provided space for one at the station.

I'm Sarah, and I offer something different: no political record to defend, but a
wealth of real-world experience and a willingness to listen.
Crowborough, where my husband also runs a small business, has been our
home for 35 years.
During my banking career I specialised in returning struggling businesses to
financial health - skills I will use to ensure our council is accountable and your
council tax isn’t wasted. I bring common sense, integrity, and a shared
commitment to our community’s future.
People in our town shouldn't be demonised for raising legitimate concerns
about their safety. I will fight to ensure that decisions adversely impacting
our community never go unchallenged.

Andrew Wilson is standing as the Conservative candidate in Crowborough South and St. Johns to ensure Crowborough has a strong, effective voice at East Sussex County Council.

He believes local decisions should be made with the community, not imposed without proper consultation. Andrew is focused on delivering competent, accountable local government that works for residents and gets the basics right.

With experience holding decision-makers to account and speaking up on issues that matter locally, he is committed to improving roads, supporting essential services, and ensuring Crowborough is properly represented.

As local government moves towards unitary councils, strong representation becomes even more important. Larger authorities risk becoming more distant, so it is vital that Crowborough has a councillor who will actively speak up for the town and ensure local priorities are not overlooked.

Andrew’s approach is practical and results-driven, prioritising the needs of residents over politics. If elected, he will work to make sure Crowborough is listened to, respected, and supported.

I am Neil Waller; I am 50 years-old and have lived in Crowborough since 1975.

I have been a Town Councillor for 24 years and a District Councillor for the past 12 years.

I am employed as a senior accountant in the NHS, working as a Deputy Director of Finance at a local mental health trust and Deputy Managing Director of the East Sussex MSK service.

I have recently completed an MBA and have obtained the highest qualification in the NHS for leadership and management.

LOCAL PROJECTS I enjoy using my skills to put something back into the community where I live, and have helped see through projects such as Walshes Park, the Community Centre, and the Basepoint Business Hub.

I ask for your support on 4th May for re-election to Crowborough Town Council and Wealden District Council to continue to represent you and work hard for a better Crowborough for all.

Please don’t hesitate to contact me with any questions.

Hello everyone! I’m Daniel, and I am proud to be standing as your Conservative candidate for the Devonshire ward in the upcoming County Council elections.

First, a quick note of transparency: if I haven’t been as visible as I’d like to be recently, it’s because I’ve had to step back temporarily to care for some family health issues. However, please know that my dedication to improving the Devonshire ward is stronger than ever.

I moved to Eastbourne from London in the summer of 2024 to be closer to work. After renting for a few months, I completely fell in love with the area, sold up in London, and made this my forever home. I have been blown away by the community spirit here. From the incredible volunteers who look after our outdoor spaces to the friendly strangers who say "good morning" when you're out for a walk—it’s a welcoming environment that is truly rare to find.

Professionally, I’ve spent 26 years in the NHS, including time as the Deputy Chief Operating Officer at the Royal Sussex County Hospital. Since 2021, I’ve been working with NHS Sussex as the Director of Elective Care with the remit to reduce patient waiting times. I recently spearheaded the Elective Care Coordination Centre—a pioneering model that helped over 6,000 patients get faster surgical treatment in its first four months (and was even showcased on BBC South East!). I was the Director of Elective and Emergency Care for NHS England between 2019 and 2021. During the pandemic I led on the serology programme nationally as part of our Covid response.
